validation/reference_data/cyclone_base/cyclone_nonlinear_chi_i_reference.json obtained published cross-code reference (Dimits et al. 2000, verified at source): offset-linear fit chi_i L_n/(rho_i^2 v_ti) = 15.4(1 - 6.0 L_T/R) giving ~2.0 at R/L_T=6.9, Dimits shift (linear critical R/L_T ~4.0, nonlinear effective ~6.0), and cross-model factors, with an explicit unit-conversion caveat to the module's chi_i_gB normalisation
entries.validity_domain: Implemented CBC machinery and schema-versioned local diagnostic evidence; the current 200-step V4 run passes linear, energy, and zonal-flow diagnostics but blocks saturated chi_i admission because it is below the minimum campaign length, outside the CBC chi_i_gB reference band, and above the tail-drift threshold. The published cross-code saturated-chi_i reference is now obtained (Dimits et al. 2000, verified at source: offset-linear fit ~2.0 gyro-Bohm at R/L_T=6.9 and the Dimits shift), and the normalisation to the module's chi_gB = rho_s^2 c_s / a is now derived (conversion factor a/L_n = 0.79137, giving a module-equivalent target chi_i_gB ~ 1.59 at R/L_T=6.9, inside the module's diagnostic band). The saturated-chi_i claim nonetheless remains blocked pending an actual long nonlinear campaign run (the current 200-step V4 run is below the saturation-evidence minimum). No published quantitative saturated chi_i claim.

entries.validity_domain: Bounded test-particle species, gyroaverage, diamagnetic-drive, velocity-grid, pitch-angle operator, and collision-coefficient reference admission with immutable reference-file SHA-256, per-case digests, explicit SI or dimensionless units, duplicate-case rejection, finite species parameters, integer velocity quadrature sizes of at least two points, and strictly ordered finite lambda grids bounded by the local trapped-passing boundary. entries.equation_contract: Runtime-admission evidence must bind the requested native execution backend, spin pacing mode, requested SNN/Z3/network/heartbeat cores, kernel realtime capability, scheduler policy, per-core CPU governor state, CPU affinity, host load, admission status, admission errors, latency statistics, benchmark command, and canonical payload SHA-256 before release evidence can rely on local runtime-admission measurements.

Repository native hardware campaign runtime-admission contract
required_actions: Capture production timing evidence only on a PREEMPT_RT or otherwise qualified realtime kernel with SCHED_FIFO or SCHED_RR execution, performance governors, hard-isolated cores, IRQ shielding, and documented host load
Keep local workstation reports in fail-closed local-regression mode with production_claim_allowed false until qualified runtime evidence is present
Keep Python, PyO3, Rust, CLI, release-evidence, and benchmark report admission semantics in parity whenever runtime-admission policy changes
entries.unit_contract: Core identifiers are non-negative CPU indices, scheduler policies use Linux scheduler names, CPU governors use sysfs governor strings, load averages are host process-queue averages, latencies are microseconds, and report and payload digests are lowercase SHA-256 hex strings.
